meta.title: AllyOS IV/IM Wellness CDS Module
meta.curated_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD (FL ME 84789) — RenuviaMD® PLLC, AllyOS Compliance Division
meta.primary_source: RenuviaMD® AllyOS Wellness IV/IM Clinical Library v2.x — MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3 (single clinical source of truth), DOC-04 v2.3 Wellness IV/IM Standing Orders, DOC-03 v2.3 Collaborative Practice Protocol, FORM-INTAKE-GFE-01 v2.0, CARD-VC v2.0, CARD-A, CARD-V v2.1, and the twelve active stack protocols. (Implemented at licensed clinics under their own standing orders; clinic and provider names are clinic-specific and are not part of this library.)
meta.scope: Adjunctive wellness IV/IM nutrient therapy, RN/ARNP-delivered under a Medical Director's signed standing orders. Adults ≥ 18 only. NOT disease treatment, diagnosis, prescription, or a medical-director relationship. The treating provider reviews, customizes, and signs.
meta.honesty_discipline: Every stack here is transcribed from the locked RenuviaMD® AllyOS wellness IV library (the primary library). Doses, routes, rates, ceilings, contraindications, and compatibility are quoted from MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3 and the stack standing orders — not from public clinic menus. Market additions that are NOT in the primary library are flagged in_primary_library:false and carry their own guardrails. Ally must say 'VERIFY — not in the locked IV library' for anything not encoded here, and never invent a dose, rate, or citation.

gate_0_gfe.name: GFE Gate-0 — legal authorization before any RN-run infusion
gate_0_gfe.rule: An RN/ARNP may NOT assign a patient to a chair or compound any bag until ALL of the following are true. This gate is scope/legal, evaluated BEFORE the clinical safety gates.
conditions_all_required: A valid Good Faith Exam / wellness eligibility evaluation is on file (FORM-INTAKE-GFE-01) and within its 12-month validity window (renew annually, or sooner on material health change, new med/allergy, adverse event, or new protocol).

Signed Medical Director standing orders authorize the requested act (DOC-04 v2.3) — either a Mode A pre-built stack (the 12 listed) administered per its published 6-step procedure, OR a Mode B custom build that has passed the 6-step pre-infusion screening in DOC-04 §2A.
A current Collaborative Practice Protocol (DOC-03 v2.3, F.S. §464.012(3)) defines the supervising-physician / ARNP relationship for the acts performed under Medical Director supervision.
FORM-02 Pre-Infusion Verify Checklist is completed THIS visit (vitals · allergy/meds review · per-visit eligibility gates) before compounding.
gate_0_gfe.scope_note: The GFE itself is a provider act. Where a clinic's APP holds independent authority (e.g., autonomous APRN registration under F.S. §464.0123) she may perform the GFE under that authority; the standing orders govern only the RN-handled IV/IM acts performed under Medical Director supervision. AllyOS treats the GFE as a separate upstream gate so the RN-run IV line never proceeds without it.
gate_0_gfe.on_fail: HOLD. Do not assign a chair or compound. Resolve the missing authorization (obtain/renew GFE, confirm standing orders cover the act, complete FORM-02) before proceeding.

vital_hold_referral_criteria.name: Universal Hold / Referral Criteria (per DOC-04 §6, DOC-03 §5)
vital_hold_referral_criteria.rule: HOLD infusion and refer to the appropriate level of care (PCP, urgent care, ED, or EMS/911) if ANY of the following is present.
criteria: SpO2 < 94 %, below known baseline, or any acute respiratory symptom (dyspnea, wheezing, cyanosis, chest pain)
Acute chest pain · acute neurologic symptoms · altered mental status
Moderate or severe dehydration · persistent vomiting/diarrhea · syncope · fever · acute infection
CHF or fluid restriction · CKD/dialysis/unknown renal status in high-risk patient
Known arrhythmia · heart block · unstable cardiac disease
Active malignancy or active oncology treatment without written oncologist clearance
Documented G6PD deficiency + Vitamin C dose > 2 g
Vitamin C 6.1–10 g without CARD-VC verbal screen documented
Any patient who does not meet the criteria of the selected active protocol
vital_hold_referral_criteria.md_consult_language_locked_verbatim: HOLD treatment. If abnormal vital signs, red-flag symptoms, or unstable clinical findings are present, the NP must defer IV therapy and refer the patient to the appropriate level of care, including PCP, urgent care, emergency department, or EMS/911 when indicated. The Medical Director should be notified for QA/protocol purposes after appropriate clinical action has been taken.

screens.screen: CARD-VC verbal screen REQUIRED for any dose 6.1–10 g (includes G6PD question). Doses ≤ 6 g use the standard intake screen. G6PD deficiency + Vit C > 2 g is an absolute hold.

Confirm no active asthma exacerbation, no sulfite sensitivity, no prior glutathione reaction. Preservative-free vial reduces but does not eliminate bronchospasm risk. IV push only — never in bag, never with Vit C.
Hold for 2nd/3rd-degree AV block, severe renal impairment (CrCl < 30), myasthenia gravis. Caution with digoxin, CCBs, neuromuscular blockers. Slow infusion ≥ 15 min minimum.
Hold for hypercalcemia, digoxin/cardiac-glycoside therapy, sarcoidosis. NOT in the same bag as Vitamin C (precipitate).
Counsel patient to hold biotin 48–72 hr before any blood lab (FDA 2017 immunoassay interference: troponin, thyroid, hCG, PTH). Document counseling.
Rate-sensitive. 250 mg first session over ≥ 2 hr; 500 mg over ≥ 3–4 hr. Dedicated bag only. Relative caution: active malignancy (theoretical), severe panic disorder.

ceilings.note: Per-session wellness ceilings (DOC-04 §3 / MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3). Above ceiling = outside the collaborative wellness scope; requires written Medical Director authorization in advance or referral to medical evaluation.

items.above_ceiling: Outside wellness scope — defer to medical evaluation

items.screen: Push 1–2 min; no piggyback, no in-bag, no slow infusion
250 mg = 2 hr first session; 500 mg = 3–4 hr first session; dedicated bag

items.above_ceiling: BMP required if > 2 g; defer to medical evaluation above scope

items.screen: Contraindicated in Leber's Hereditary Optic Neuropathy

ingredients.name: 0.9% Normal Saline (default base fluid)
ingredients.standard: 250/500 mL (500 mL default for most stacks); 1 L for hydration only
ingredients.absolute_ci: Decompensated CHF · pulmonary edema · severe hypernatremia
ingredients.relative_ci: ESRD on dialysis · CHF (smaller volume) · severe hypertension

ingredients.name: Lactated Ringer's (alternate base)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hyperkalemia · severe hepatic disease · concurrent ceftriaxone
ingredients.note: Do NOT use as base for high-dose Vit C bags — prefer NS

ingredients.name: Vita Complex (B-Complex injection)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Known hypersensitivity to any B vitamin (rare)

ingredients.name: Methylcobalamin (Methyl B12) — packet standard
ingredients.route: IV push (1–3 min slow), IM, or in bag
ingredients.absolute_ci: Leber's Hereditary Optic Neuropathy (LHON)
ingredients.note: Methylcobalamin only — no cyanocobalamin or hydroxocobalamin. Ready-to-use only.

ingredients.name: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C) — preservative-free
ingredients.rate: ≤ 0.5 g/min for 5–10 g (typically 30–60 min for 10 g)
ingredients.absolute_ci: G6PD deficiency · ESRD/dialysis · active hemochromatosis · bortezomib (Velcade) · pregnancy > 2 g
ingredients.interactions: Bortezomib (CONTRAINDICATED) · methotrexate · oral iron · NOT compatible with Calcium Gluconate in same bag
ingredients.adverse: Vein irritation (slow rate) · transient flushing · false-high glucose on finger-stick meters
ingredients.citation: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE 5(7):e11414; (2006) CMAJ 174(7):937

ingredients.name: Biotin
ingredients.interactions: FDA 2017 ALERT: immunoassay interference (troponin, thyroid, hCG, PTH). Hold 48–72 hr before any blood lab.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Chloride
ingredients.rate: Slow over 15–30 min minimum (rapid push → flushing, hypotension, AV block)
ingredients.absolute_ci: 2nd/3rd-degree AV block · severe renal impairment (CrCl < 30) · myasthenia gravis
ingredients.antidote: Calcium Gluconate 1 g IV slow is the antidote for Mg toxicity

ingredients.name: Calcium Gluconate — preservative-free
ingredients.route: IV in bag (slow) OR Y-port after Vit C bag
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hypercalcemia · digoxin therapy · ventricular fibrillation · sarcoidosis
ingredients.interactions: NOT compatible with Vitamin C in same bag (calcium ascorbate precipitate) · digoxin potentiation · ceftriaxone precipitate

ingredients.name: Zinc Sulfate
ingredients.absolute_ci: Severe copper deficiency · prior zinc toxicity

ingredients.name: Selenium (Sodium Selenite / Selenious Acid)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Selenium toxicity history

ingredients.name: Trace Mineral Injection (Zn + Cu + Mn + Se)
ingredients.composition: Zinc 3 mg + Copper 0.3 mg + Manganese 55 mcg + Selenium 60 mcg per mL
ingredients.note: Do not stack with separate Zn/Se same session (double-dosing). Cu/Mn accumulate — frequency cap once every 4 weeks.

ingredients.name: L-Taurine
ingredients.citation: Schaffer S, Kim HW (2018) Biomol Ther 26(3):225

ingredients.name: L-Carnitine
ingredients.adverse: Mild GI · fishy body odor (high doses, benign)
ingredients.citation: Pekala J et al. (2011) Curr Drug Metab 12(7):667

ingredients.name: L-Glutamine
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hepatic encephalopathy

ingredients.name: Amino Blend (Proline + Arginine + Lysine + Citrulline)
ingredients.absolute_ci: PKU (if phenylalanine present) · severe renal impairment

ingredients.name: Glutathione (reduced) — preservative-free
ingredients.standard: 600–1,200 mg slow IV push over 1–2 min
ingredients.route: IV PUSH ONLY (post-drip). No in-bag mixing, no piggyback, no slow infusion.
ingredients.administration: Separate syringe through Y-port closest to catheter; flush line 3–5 mL NS BEFORE and 3 mL AFTER
ingredients.absolute_ci: Active asthma exacerbation
ingredients.relative_ci: Severe asthma (controlled) · prior glutathione reaction · sulfite sensitivity
ingredients.interactions: NOT compatible with Vitamin C in same bag/syringe (redox inactivates glutathione)
ingredients.above_ceiling: Aesthetic skin-lightening NOT recommended (FDA 2015 alert: SJS/TEN, renal failure at ≥ 2 g)
ingredients.citation: Hauser RA et al. (2009) Mov Disord 24(7):979; FDA 2015 IV Glutathione Aesthetic Alert

ingredients.name: NAD+ — ready-to-use
ingredients.route: IV slow infusion in dedicated 250–500 mL NS bag (never mixed with other ingredients)
ingredients.rate: 250 mg: ≥ 2 hr first session (90–120 min tolerant); 500 mg: ≥ 3–4 hr. Start 2 mg/min; titrate to 4–5 mg/min only if asymptomatic at 30 min. Rapid infusion → chest pressure, GI cramping, anxiety, flushing.
ingredients.absolute_ci: Known NAD+ hypersensitivity
ingredients.note: Ready-to-use only — not lyophilized. Optional PO Tylenol 30 min prior for new patients.
ingredients.citation: Grant R et al. (2019) Front Aging Neurosci 11:257; Brakedal B et al. (2022) Cell Metab 34(3):396

ingredients.name: Lipotropic IM (Methionine + Inositol + Choline + Methylcobalamin, methyl-only)
ingredients.composition: Methionine 12.4 + Inositol 25 + Choline 25 + B6 2 mg/mL + separate Methylcobalamin
ingredients.absolute_ci: Active gallbladder disease · severe renal impairment · pregnancy

ingredients.name: N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC) — IM only at wellness dose
ingredients.route: IM only (high-dose IV NAC is hospital-level, not wellness scope)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Active asthma exacerbation · prior NAC reaction

ingredients.name: Coenzyme Q10 (Ubidecarenone) — IM only
ingredients.route: IM ONLY (grapeseed oil base — IV not authorized: fat-embolism risk)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hypersensitivity to grapeseed oil

ingredients.name: Cyanocobalamin (B12)
ingredients.route: IV push, IM, or in bag
ingredients.relative_ci: Leber's Hereditary Optic Neuropathy / tobacco amblyopia (cyano form worse — methyl/hydroxo preferred)
ingredients.note: Generic-menu item. The original wellness standing-orders packet used Methylcobalamin only; AllyOS lists cyano because it is commonly stocked, with the methyl-preferred caution attached.

ingredients.name: Hydroxocobalamin (B12)
ingredients.route: IV push, IM, or in bag
ingredients.adverse: Expected reddish/pink urine and skin discoloration (chromaturia); at high IV doses can interfere with colorimetric lab assays (creatinine, AST, bilirubin) and colorimetric hemodialysis monitors
ingredients.note: Generic-menu item; longer-acting B12 form. Packet standard is methylcobalamin; hydroxo is acceptable, listed because commonly stocked.

ingredients.name: Pyridoxine (Vitamin B6) — standalone
ingredients.adverse: Peripheral neuropathy at chronic high doses (well above wellness)

ingredients.name: Vitamin D3 (Cholecalciferol) — IM only
ingredients.cadence: Monthly or per patient's D3 level
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hypercalcemia · vitamin D toxicity · sarcoidosis

ingredients.name: Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA)
ingredients.absolute_ci: History of insulin autoimmune syndrome (Hirata disease) or prior ALA-associated hypoglycemia
ingredients.relative_ci: Diabetes / insulin or sulfonylurea use (additive hypoglycemia) · thiamine deficiency (co-repletes thiamine; ALA increases thiamine demand) · East Asian ancestry / HLA-DRB1*04:06 or *04:03 (IAS predisposition)
ingredients.guardrail: PRIMARY RISK — INSULIN AUTOIMMUNE SYNDROME (Hirata disease): ALA is an established trigger and can cause severe spontaneous hypoglycemia, with predisposition in HLA-DRB1*04:06 (Asian) and *04:03 (European) backgrounds. Screen for hypoglycemia risk and prior reaction; counsel. Give with thiamine repletion. The original wellness packet EXCLUDED ALA for this reason; AllyOS lists it because it is stocked, with this caution prominent.

ingredients.name: Calcium Chloride
ingredients.standard: Typically lower volume than gluconate (≈3× elemental Ca of gluconate)
ingredients.absolute_ci: Hypercalcemia · digoxin therapy · ventricular fibrillation · sarcoidosis
ingredients.guardrail: More sclerosing / vesicant than Calcium Gluconate — high extravasation injury risk peripherally. For peripheral wellness IV, Calcium GLUCONATE is preferred. NOT in the same bag as Vitamin C (precipitate). Hold if digoxin/cardiac glycoside.

ingredients.name: L-Arginine
ingredients.standard: Component of Amino Blend; standalone available
ingredients.absolute_ci: Severe renal impairment (arginine HCl can cause life-threatening HYPERKALEMIA via transcellular potassium shift) · severe hepatic impairment
ingredients.relative_ci: Recent MI · hypotension (vasodilator) · herpes (theoretical) · urea-cycle / arginine-metabolism disorders
ingredients.adverse: Vasodilation / hypotension at higher doses; hyperkalemia (HCl salt)
ingredients.guardrail: Screen renal function before IV arginine — the HCl salt shifts potassium extracellularly and has caused fatal hyperkalemia in renal impairment.

ingredients.name: L-Proline
ingredients.standard: Component of Amino Blend; standalone available
ingredients.note: Collagen-synthesis amino acid; part of the Amino Blend.

ingredients.name: Glycine
ingredients.standard: Commonly a buffer/adjunct in compounded blends
ingredients.route: IV in bag / component of compounded injectables
ingredients.note: Appears as a stabilizer/adjunct in some compounded blends (e.g., GLP-1 + glycine + B12). Generally inert at wellness amounts.

ingredients.name: Mineral Blend (composite)
ingredients.composition: Multi-mineral compounded blend (varies by supplier — confirm components and per-mL content against the actual label)
ingredients.guardrail: Composite — do NOT co-administer with standalone forms of the same minerals (double-dosing). Verify exact composition on the vial before stacking.

ingredients.name: Tri-Immune Boost (composite: Zinc + Ascorbic Acid + Glutathione)
ingredients.composition: Zinc + Vitamin C + Glutathione (premade immune blend)
ingredients.route: IV in bag — NOTE: contains glutathione; standalone glutathione is otherwise IV-push-only. Confirm the premade blend's stability/labeling.
ingredients.guardrail: Contains Vitamin C AND glutathione together as a manufactured blend — this is a vendor-formulated exception to the in-bag glutathione+Vit C incompatibility; do NOT replicate by hand-mixing glutathione into a Vit C bag. Do not double-dose Zn/Vit C/glutathione from other components.

ingredients.name: MIC / MICC / Lipo-MIC family (Methionine + Inositol + Choline ± Carnitine/B12) — IM
ingredients.absolute_ci: Active gallbladder disease · severe renal impairment · pregnancy
ingredients.note: Lipotropic family — same class as the locked Lipotropic IM. Many vendor variants (Lipo-Mino, Lipo Plus, LipoStat) — verify the exact formula on the label.

ingredients.name: Methylene Blue — HIGH-CAUTION
ingredients.absolute_ci: Concurrent OR recent (within ~2 weeks / 5 half-lives, washout) serotonergic agents — SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, TCAs, triptans, bupropion, buspirone, dextromethorphan, AND serotonergic opioids (tramadol, meperidine, fentanyl, methadone) (SEROTONIN SYNDROME) · G6PD deficiency (hemolysis) · pregnancy · severe renal impairment
ingredients.guardrail: SAFETY-CRITICAL. Methylene Blue is a potent MAO-A inhibitor — combined with serotonergic drugs it can cause life-threatening serotonin syndrome. Screen the FULL medication list BEFORE use — not only antidepressants but also tramadol/meperidine/fentanyl/methadone, dextromethorphan, and bupropion — and screen for use within the prior ~2 weeks, not just same-day. Screen G6PD (hemolysis risk). Causes blue-green urine/secretions (expected) and can transiently and falsely LOWER pulse-oximeter SpO2 readings. Not a routine wellness nutrient — treat as a high-caution specialty agent requiring explicit screening.
ingredients.interactions: Serotonergic agents incl. serotonergic opioids — tramadol/meperidine/fentanyl/methadone, dextromethorphan, bupropion (CONTRAINDICATED, concurrent or recent) · G6PD (hemolysis) · pulse-oximetry interference

ingredients.name: Ondansetron (Zofran) — anti-nausea adjunct
ingredients.standard: 4 mg IV push (slow 30 sec) or IM
ingredients.absolute_ci: Concurrent apomorphine · long QT syndrome
ingredients.note: Administration adjunct (also in the emergency/PRN cart), not a wellness stack ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ketorolac (Toradol) — Rx pain adjunct
ingredients.absolute_ci: Active GI bleed/ulcer · renal impairment · hypovolemia / volume depletion · bleeding disorder / anticoagulation · NSAID or aspirin allergy · labor and delivery · pregnancy (3rd trimester) · peri-operative CABG · breastfeeding
ingredients.note: Rx adjunct, not a wellness nutrient. Provider-prescribed; short-course only — max 5 days combined (IV/IM + oral).
ingredients.source: Generic IV/IM master menu (clinic inventory — Rx adjunct)

ingredients.name: Famotidine (Pepcid) — Rx adjunct
ingredients.guardrail: Renally cleared — reduce dose in renal impairment (CrCl <50; CNS effects/confusion, esp. elderly); avoid rapid IV push (rare QT prolongation in renal impairment — give slow or diluted).
ingredients.note: H2 blocker; allergic-reaction adjunct (often with diphenhydramine). Rx adjunct, not a wellness nutrient.
ingredients.source: Generic IV/IM master menu (clinic inventory — Rx adjunct)

line_crossover.note: Agents sometimes seen on clinic order sheets that are NOT part of the IV/IM wellness bar and are NOT inventoried as office stock. The IV bar runs on 503B office-use stock (generic vials, administered chairside). The agents below are 503A patient-specific prescriptions, direct-shipped to the patient and self-administered — a provider Rx workflow, not chairside bar stock. AllyOS handles them in the Peptide module (prescribing decision-support), never the IV inventory or builder, so guardrails are not split.

compliance_distinction.iv_bar: 503B outsourcing-facility office-use stock — generic vials kept and administered at the clinic (this IV/IM module).
compliance_distinction.peptides: 503A patient-specific compounded prescriptions — prescribed by the provider, shipped direct to the patient, self-administered at home. NOT inventoried by the clinic.

entries.handling: 503A patient-specific, direct-to-patient shipping, provider-prescribed (Rx) — do NOT inventory. Not a chairside IV/IM bar agent.
entries.route_to: AllyOS peptide layers (interactions.json, peptide-interactions.json, patient-factor-rules.json, monitoring-map.json).

entries.handling: 503A patient-specific, direct-to-patient shipping, provider-prescribed (Rx) — do NOT inventory.

principles: AllyOS holds the GENERIC clinical palette only — agent, concentration/size, typical dose range, route, ceiling, and safety guardrails. It carries NO pharmacy name, brand, SKU, cost, or stock level. Sourcing, pricing, and inventory live in the clinic's own inventory tool and are the clinic/provider's decision.

The menu lists what is COMMONLY STOCKED across IV bars (the inventory is nearly identical everywhere), including agents the original wellness standing-orders packet chose to exclude (e.g., Alpha Lipoic Acid, cyano/hydroxo B12). Those are included WITH their guardrail so the provider sees the caution rather than having the agent silently omitted.

Provider-driven: the provider selects and NAMES their own stacks from this palette (Mode B custom builds). AllyOS supplies the live safety/compatibility/ceiling/patient-factor logic and an advisory verdict — it does not own or mandate the formulary.
AllyOS advises and discloses; it does not govern. No agent is blocked — high-caution agents (Methylene Blue, ALA) surface strong advisories the provider acts on and signs.
Composite/premade blends (Amino Blend, Mineral Blend, Tri-Immune, MIC family, Myers premade) must have their exact label composition confirmed before stacking, to avoid double-dosing a component.

stacks.title: Myers Cocktail (Standard Base Stack)
stacks.indication: General wellness optimization · energy/fatigue support · mild dehydration support · post-exercise recovery · foundational micronutrient replenishment. Most common starting point for new wellness IV patients.
stacks.scope_limitation: Adjunctive nutritional wellness support. Not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, prevent, reverse, or manage any disease. Adults ≥ 18.
stacks.base: 0.9% NS 500 mL (250 mL acceptable for short visits)

optional_add_ons.note: Optional only · NOT in same bag as Vit C · separate after Vit C bag with line flush, or separate piggyback

stacks.infusion_time: 45–60 min (500 mL); Mg total time ≥ 15 min
stacks.frequency: No more than once weekly for up to 4 initial sessions, then every 2–4 weeks; reassess for continued frequent use
citations: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE
Carmel R (2008) Blood
Berger MM ESPEN
USP <797> Immediate-Use

stacks.title: Longevity Stack (NAD+ + Glutathione)
stacks.indication: Cellular energy support · methylation/redox balance · oxidative stress support · adjunctive wellness in healthy adults.
stacks.scope_limitation: Adjunctive wellness only. Not authorized for neurodegenerative, oncologic, metabolic, or psychiatric disease. Adult ≥ 18.

components.note: Rate-sensitive: 250 mg over ≥ 2 hr first session; 90–120 min tolerant. 500 mg over ≥ 3–4 hr.

post_drip.note: Post-drip only after NAD+ bag complete + symptom-free 5 min; flush 10 mL NS before push

stacks.infusion_time: NAD+ rate-driven (2–4 hr per dose); glutathione push after
key_gates: No active malignancy/pregnancy/breastfeeding without separate evaluation
NAD+ rate titration + 50% slow on chest tightness/flushing/cramping/anxiety
citations: Grant R et al. (2019) Front Aging Neurosci 11:257
Brakedal B et al. (2022) Cell Metab 34(3):396
Hauser RA et al. (2009) Mov Disord
FDA 2015 IV Glutathione aesthetic alert
Mestayer P (Springfield Wellness)

stacks.title: Beauty / Glow Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive antioxidant support · skin/hair/nail support · general wellness.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for dermatologic disease, alopecia, or hyperpigmentation. Adult ≥ 18.

components.note: FDA 2017 lab-interference counseling; hold 48–72 hr before labs

stacks.infusion_time: Main bag 30–45 min; glutathione push after
key_gates: CARD-VC if Vit C raised > 6 g
citations: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE
FDA 2017 Biotin alert
FDA 2015 IV Glutathione aesthetic alert

stacks.title: Defense / Immune Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive immune support · antioxidant load support · wellness during high-exposure periods.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for acute infection, immunodeficiency, or disease. Active infection/febrile illness: defer. Adult ≥ 18.

components.note: CARD-VC verbal screen MANDATORY at > 6 g; up to 10 g ceiling

post_drip.dose: 200 mg standard, up to 1,200 mg if boost requested
post_drip.note: Mandatory post-drip push — never in bag with Vit C (redox)

stacks.infusion_time: Main bag 30–45 min; glutathione push after
citations: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE
Hauser RA et al. (2009) Mov Disord

stacks.title: Fast Recovery Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive post-exercise recovery · post-illness rebound · athletic recovery · acute fatigue support.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for chronic fatigue syndrome, post-viral syndromes, or disease. Active fever/infection/symptomatic dehydration: defer. Adult ≥ 18.
stacks.base: 0.9% NS (or LR) 500 mL (1 L if dehydration support indicated)

components.dose: 6 g (no CARD-VC at 6 g; CARD-VC if > 6 g)

post_drip.note: Recommended for athletic recovery; defer if asthma/atopy

citations: Mah E et al. (2018) Nutrients
Pekala J et al. (2011) Curr Drug Metab

stacks.title: Neuro / Brain Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive cognitive support · focus support · neurologic support · general wellness.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for dementia, Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, MS, depression, anxiety, ADHD, or any psychiatric/neurologic disease. Adult ≥ 18.
stacks.note: NAD+ is NOT included in IV-07. For NAD+ support schedule IV-03 (250 mg) or WEL-06 (500 mg) as a dedicated visit.
key_gates: High-dose B12 may cause transient anxiety in MTHFR-variant
citations: MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3

stacks.title: Metabolic Burner (MIC) Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive metabolic/lipotropic support · energy support · adjunctive support for lifestyle-based weight management.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for obesity, metabolic syndrome, diabetes, NAFLD, or disease. Not a substitute for diet/exercise/medical weight management. Defer in pregnancy, breastfeeding, active eating disorder, severe gallbladder disease, sulfite sensitivity. Adult ≥ 18.

same_visit_add_ons.note: Signature ingredient; separate IM injection same visit; separate sharps; documented same FORM-05

citations: Pooyandjoo M et al. (2016) Obes Rev

stacks.title: Detox / Liver Support Stack
stacks.indication: Adjunctive hepatic/antioxidant support · oxidative stress support · general wellness.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for hepatitis (viral/autoimmune/alcoholic/NAFLD/NASH), cirrhosis, HCC, DILI, or hepatic disease. Active decompensation/new jaundice: defer until medical clearance. Adult ≥ 18.
stacks.infusion_time: Main bag 45–60 min; Mg total time ≥ 15 min
key_gates: Defer if hepatic decompensation/jaundice/known liver disease
citations: Hauser RA et al. (2009) Mov Disord
FDA 2015 IV Glutathione aesthetic alert

stacks.title: Trace Mineral IV
stacks.indication: Adjunctive trace mineral support · nutritional repletion in adults with documented/suspected suboptimal intake.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for deficiency disease (Wilson's, Menkes, acrodermatitis enteropathica). Frequency limit: no more than once every 4 weeks (Cu/Mn accumulate). Defer in Wilson's, cholestatic liver disease, ESRD/dialysis, pregnancy. Adult ≥ 18.

components.ingredient: Trace Mineral Injection (Zn + Cu + Mn + Se)
components.note: Composition per MASTER-MENU §3.3 (Zn 3 mg + Cu 0.3 mg + Mn 55 mcg + Se 60 mcg/mL). The IV-11 standing-order label also printed 'Cr'; MASTER-MENU governs as the single source of clinical truth.

stacks.frequency: Max once every 4 weeks — document last trace-mineral infusion date before initiating
citations: Berger MM ESPEN trace element guidelines

stacks.title: High-Dose NAD+ IV
stacks.indication: High-dose adjunctive NAD+ support for cellular energy/longevity/cognitive support. Reserved for tolerant established patients.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for neurodegenerative disease, addiction recovery, oncologic disease, or any diagnosis. First-session patients must use IV-03 250 mg first; WEL-06 500 mg only after documented 250 mg tolerance. Adult ≥ 18.
stacks.base: 0.9% NS 500 mL dedicated bag (NAD+ only — never mixed)

components.note: Rate: 500 mg over ≥ 3–4 hr first session, 2–3 hr subsequent. Never faster. Dedicated bag.

stacks.infusion_time: 3–4 hr first session (chair-time intensive — verify chair availability before scheduling)
key_gates: Must have documented 250 mg (IV-03) tolerance first
NAD+ rate titration + 50% slow on symptoms
No active malignancy/pregnancy/breastfeeding without separate evaluation
citations: Grant R et al. (2019) Front Aging Neurosci 11:257
Brakedal B et al. (2022) Cell Metab 34(3):396
Mestayer P (Springfield Wellness)

stacks.title: Amino Blend (Standalone)
stacks.indication: Adjunctive amino acid support · post-exertion recovery support · general wellness for adults in physical training or post-illness recovery.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for sarcopenia, cachexia, malnutrition syndromes, or disease. Defer in active hepatic disease (urea cycle stress), pregnancy, breastfeeding, known protein-metabolism disorder. Adult ≥ 18.
citations: Standard amino-acid IV nutrition references

stacks.title: Glutathione IV Push (Standalone)
stacks.indication: Standalone Glutathione IV push for antioxidant/hepatic/oxidative-stress support — short-duration visit, no main bag.
stacks.scope_limitation: Wellness adjunct only. Not for liver, neurodegenerative, or dermatologic disease. Aesthetic skin-lightening NOT recommended (FDA 2015 alert: SJS/TEN, renal failure at ≥ 2 g). Defer in active asthma exacerbation, prior glutathione hypersensitivity, sulfite sensitivity. Adult ≥ 18.
stacks.base: 0.9% NS 100 mL (line patency only) OR straight venipuncture with NS flush

components.note: Signature ingredient; via Y-port closest to catheter; flush 3–5 mL NS before, 3 mL after; never in bag/piggyback/slow infusion

stacks.infusion_time: ~10–15 min total (access · push · 5 min post-push observation)
key_gates: Pre-push screen: no active wheeze/new rash/recent allergic reaction
citations: Hauser RA et al. (2009) Mov Disord
FDA 2015 IV Glutathione aesthetic alert

compatibility_matrix.note: Key incompatibilities, transcribed verbatim from MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3 Compatibility Matrix.

rules.action: Give Calcium separately after Vit C bag with line flush, or separate piggyback
Glutathione is post-drip IV push only, after line flush

rules.combination: Trace Mineral Injection + standalone Zn or Se (same session)

rules.rationale: Use NS as base for Vit C bags

packet_standards: Methylcobalamin is the only B12 form used (no cyanocobalamin or hydroxocobalamin).
Alpha Lipoic Acid (ALA) is NOT used (reported insulin autoimmune syndrome risk).
Ready-to-use solutions only — no lyophilized vials requiring reconstitution.
Preservative-free defaults: Vitamin C, Glutathione, Calcium Gluconate.
USP <797> immediate-use, single-patient, 4-hour beyond-use date; no batching/storage.

emergency_cart.note: Mandatory chairside per DOC-04 §5; expiration checked monthly (DOC-07).

items.indication: Anaphylaxis — 0.3 mg IM lateral thigh (CARD-A); repeat q5–15 min ×2 PRN
Anaphylaxis adjunct — 40–125 mg IV/IM (post-Epi; NOT first-line)

items.form: Oral gel 15 g + IV D50 if needed

chairside_cards: CARD-VC Vit C Verbal Screen v2.0
emergency_cart.escalation_rule: Loss of consciousness > 1 min OR anaphylactoid features: CALL 911 · ACTIVATE EMS · DO NOT DELAY FOR MD CONSULTATION. Notify Medical Director for QA after clinical action.

CARD_VC.name: Vitamin C Verbal Screen (CARD-VC v2.0) — chairside dose-tiered gate

tiers.screen: No formal verbal screen — standard intake screen applies
MANDATORY 9-question verbal screen, including G6PD status. If G6PD-deficient → cap at 2 g or decline. Document in FORM-05.
Out of wellness scope — defer to medical evaluation

citations: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE
AANP IV Nutrient Therapy Position Paper

forms.when: First visit; renew annually or on material health change. Valid 12 months. (GFE Gate-0)
BEFORE every infusion (vitals · allergy/meds · eligibility gates)

forms.form: FORM-05 Wellness IV Infusion Monitoring Sheet
forms.when: EVERY infusion (pre-infusion · infusion details · symptoms/interventions · post/discharge)
ONLY when an AE occurs (within 24 hr; ARNP + Medical Director co-signed)
Documented for every Vit C 6.1–10 g dose

market_additions.note: Items requested for the menu that are NOT in the locked primary library. Flagged in_primary_library:false. They do not inherit primary-library grounding and carry their own guardrails. Ally must disclose that these are additions to the primary library, not part of it.

entries.name: Niagen / Nicotinamide Riboside (NR / NRC) — NAD+ PRECURSOR (distinct from NAD+)
entries.category: NAD+ precursor (NOT the same agent as IV NAD+ in IV-03/WEL-06)
entries.clinical_positioning: Niagen is nicotinamide riboside, a precursor the body converts toward NAD+. It is pharmacologically DISTINCT from the ready-to-use NAD+ used in IV-03/WEL-06. Do not present NR and NAD+ as interchangeable, and do not claim NR delivers the same clinical effect as direct IV NAD+.
do_not_claim: Do NOT claim NR/Niagen is a proven peptide synergy or that it potentiates any peptide — no direct-combination evidence exists.
Do NOT claim NR is equivalent or superior to IV NAD+ clinically.
Do NOT claim disease treatment (longevity/anti-aging/neuro claims are not authorized).
entries.allowed_language: Adjunctive NAD+ precursor wellness support; cellular-energy support language only; wellness-framed, not disease-framed.

regulatory_sourcing_layer.fda_status: Nicotinamide riboside is NOT an FDA-approved drug (no NDA). Oral NR (Niagen) holds GRAS/NDI supplement status. Injectable/IV NR is NOT an approved drug product.
regulatory_sourcing_layer.compounding_pathway: FDA permits nicotinamide riboside chloride (NRC) for compounding by 503B outsourcing facilities — NR is on FDA's Category I interim list of bulk substances usable in 503B compounding (a USP NRCl monograph exists). Pharmaceutical-grade injectable/IV NR is therefore obtained as a compounded preparation from an FDA-registered 503B outsourcing facility.
regulatory_sourcing_layer.sourcing_disclaimer: ADVISORY / DISCLAIMER ONLY — AllyOS does NOT regulate, restrict, mandate, or approve sourcing. Informational context the provider may consider: injectable NR is commonly obtained as a 503B-compounded preparation, and 2025 FDA enforcement focused on NON-COMPLIANT compounding prepared without prescriber oversight. Confirming a source's compliance, holding a valid prescription, and documenting the practitioner-patient relationship are the provider's decisions and responsibility. The clinic/provider chooses the supplier and how much inventory to carry.
regulatory_sourcing_layer.allyos_posture: AllyOS is clinical decision-support + disclaimer, NOT governance. It names no supplier, mandates no source, and enforces nothing about sourcing. The provider/clinic owns every sourcing and inventory decision. Reference compounders (e.g. 503B outsourcing facilities, generic-vial suppliers) may be mentioned by the provider; AllyOS neither endorses nor verifies any of them.
regulatory_sourcing_layer.enforcement_note: 2025 FDA enforcement targeted NON-COMPLIANT 503B compounding of IV NAD+/NR prepared without proper prescriber oversight. Compliant, prescriber-supervised, 503B-sourced administration by a licensed practitioner is the lawful pathway.
active_trials: NCT06382688 (IV NR vs NAD+, registered on ClinicalTrials.gov)
Additional SC/IM NR trials are registered — confirm current NCT IDs on ClinicalTrials.gov before citing
regulatory_sourcing_layer.verify_language: Regulatory and sourcing status changes — Ally must tell the provider to confirm current FDA 503B Category-I status and verify their chosen 503B facility's compliance before relying on this. AllyOS does not endorse any facility.

entries.safety_inherits: If administered IV/IM in the wellness setting, apply the same GFE Gate-0, vital hold/referral criteria, dedicated-bag handling (treat like NAD+: rate-sensitive, dedicated bag, never co-mixed), and emergency-cart readiness as the primary-library NAD+ protocols (IV-03 / WEL-06), until a dedicated NR standing order is authored and locked.
entries.status: PROPOSED — not yet a locked standing order in the primary library. Requires a Medical Director-authored NR standing order + AllyOS auditor lock before it fires as an authorized stack.

rules: Wellness framing only. Every stack is adjunctive nutritional/wellness support — never diagnosis, treatment, cure, prevention, reversal, or management of disease.
Decision-support, not a prescription, standing order, or medical-director relationship. The treating provider reviews, customizes, and signs.
GFE Gate-0 is upstream of clinical gates: no chair assignment or compounding without a valid GFE + signed standing orders + FORM-02 this visit.
Glutathione is IV push only (post-drip); never in a bag, never with Vit C, never piggyback, never slow infusion.
NAD+ is dedicated-bag only, rate-sensitive; never co-mixed.

Niagen/NR is a distinct NAD+ precursor, not IV NAD+, and not a proven peptide synergy. On sourcing, AllyOS advises and discloses but does not govern: it may note that injectable NR is commonly 503B-compounded and that a valid prescription / prescriber oversight applies, but it names no supplier and the sourcing decision is the provider's.

Never invent a dose, rate, ceiling, or citation. Anything not in this locked module or the MASTER-MENU: respond 'VERIFY — not in the locked IV library; confirm against MASTER-MENU-01 / primary sources before relying on it.'

validation_cases.scenario: RN wants to start IV-01 but the patient's GFE is 14 months old.
validation_cases.expected: GFE Gate-0 fails (outside 12-month validity). HOLD chair assignment; renew GFE before proceeding.

validation_cases.scenario: Patient requests IV-05 with Vit C 7.5 g; intake notes possible G6PD deficiency.
validation_cases.expected: CARD-VC mandatory (> 6 g). If G6PD-deficient, cap at 2 g or decline; Vit C > 2 g with G6PD is an absolute hold.

validation_cases.scenario: Provider asks if glutathione can go in the Vit C bag to save time.
validation_cases.expected: INCOMPATIBLE (redox inactivates glutathione). Glutathione is post-drip IV push only, after line flush.

validation_cases.scenario: Myers with optional Calcium — mix all in one bag?
validation_cases.expected: Calcium Gluconate NOT in same bag as Vit C (precipitate). Give separately after Vit C bag with line flush or separate piggyback.

validation_cases.scenario: First-time patient wants WEL-06 500 mg NAD+ today.
validation_cases.expected: Not recommended first session — requires documented 250 mg (IV-03) tolerance first; 500 mg infuses over ≥ 3–4 hr in a dedicated bag.

validation_cases.scenario: Provider asks Ally to add 'NAD+ (Niagen)' to a peptide stack as proven synergy.
validation_cases.expected: Niagen/NR is a distinct NAD+ precursor, NOT IV NAD+, and there is no direct-combination/peptide-synergy evidence. Disclose it is a market addition (not in the locked primary library). On sourcing, advise + disclaim only (not govern): note that injectable NR is commonly 503B-compounded and a valid prescription applies, but name no supplier and leave the choice to the provider; flag VERIFY current FDA 503B status.

validation_cases.scenario: Patient on IV-04 (Biotin 10 mg) has thyroid labs scheduled in 2 days.
validation_cases.expected: Counsel to hold biotin 48–72 hr before labs (FDA 2017 immunoassay interference); document counseling.

validation_cases.scenario: Patient wants IV-11 Trace Mineral weekly.
validation_cases.expected: Frequency cap once every 4 weeks (Cu/Mn accumulation). Document last trace-mineral infusion date before initiating.

validation_cases.scenario: Provider asks for an IV ozone or chelation EDTA protocol.
validation_cases.expected: VERIFY — not in the locked IV library. Not part of MASTER-MENU-01; defer to primary sources / Medical Director before relying on it.
